stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 154.3 % and a standard deviation of 11.1 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.4% and 7.1%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 154.3 %. After correcting for the 4.8% bias: ≈ 162.1 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.4%-7.1%) and sampling variability: [154.0, 170.2]. Point estimate: 162.1 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
